title: Binomial flows: Denoising and flow matching for discrete ordinal data
summary: Flow-based generative modeling in continuous spaces exploit Tweedie's formula to express the denoiser (learned in training) as a score function (used in sampling). In contrast, this relation has been largely missing in the discrete setting where common approaches focus on learning discrete scores and rates. In this work we close this gap for discrete non-negative ordinal data by introducing Binomial flows. Our framework provides a simple recipe for training a discrete diffusion model which simultaneously denoises, samples, and estimates exact likelihoods. We verify our methodology on synthe...

metadata.comment: Establishes a denoiser-score relation for discrete ordinal data through binomial flows, unifying training and sampling.

title: Borrowed Geometry: Computational Reuse of Frozen Text-Pretrained Transformer Weights Across Modalities
summary: Frozen Gemma 4 31B weights pretrained exclusively on text tokens, unmodified, transfer across modality boundaries through a thin trainable interface. (1) OGBench scene-play-singletask-task1-v0: $+4.33$pt over published GCIQL at $n=3$ with std 0.74 -- a published-SOTA win on a robotic manipulation task the substrate has never seen. (2) D4RL Walker2d-medium-v2: Decision-Transformer parity ($76.2 \pm 0.8$, $n=3$) at $0.43\times$ DT's trainable count, with the frozen substrate compressing to a 5L slice ($+1.66$pt over the 6L baseline at $n=3$). (3) Associative recall as the cleanest pretraining...

metadata.comment: Shows frozen text-pretrained transformer slices can transfer across modalities through a thin interface, with head-level mechanistic probes identifying reused circuits.

title: Consistent Diffusion Language Models
summary: Diffusion language models (DLMs) are an attractive alternative to autoregressive models because they promise sublinear-time, parallel generation, yet practical gains remain elusive as high-quality samples still demand hundreds of refinement steps. In continuous domains, consistency training along the probability-flow ODE is a popular recipe to accelerate diffusion. For discrete diffusion, no analogous sample-space ODE exists, making direct adaptation ill-defined. We argue that the natural discrete substitute is not a deterministic trajectory but its stochastic counterpart: the exact posteri...

metadata.comment: Develops a discrete consistency-training principle for diffusion language models using exact posterior bridges, improving few-step generation.

title: Hyperspherical Forward-Forward with Prototypical Representations
summary: The Forward-Forward (FF) algorithm presents a compelling, bio-inspired alternative to backpropagation. However, while efficient in training, it has a computationally prohibitive inference process that requires a separate forward pass for every class that is evaluated. In this work, we introduce the Hyperspherical Forward-Forward (HFF), a novel reformulation that resolves this critical bottleneck. Our core innovation is to reframe the local objective of each layer from a binary goodness-of-fit task to a direct multi-class classification problem within a hyperspherical feature space. We achie...

metadata.comment: Reformulates Forward-Forward into hyperspherical prototype-based local learning with single-pass inference, removing FF's main computational bottleneck.

title: NonZero: Interaction-Guided Exploration for Multi-Agent Monte Carlo Tree Search
summary: Monte Carlo Tree Search (MCTS) scales poorly in cooperative multi-agent domains because expansion must consider an exponentially large set of joint actions, severely limiting exploration under realistic search budgets. We propose NonZero, which keeps multi-agent MCTS tractable by running surrogate-guided selection over a low-dimensional nonlinear representation using an interaction-guided proposal rule, instead of directly exploring the full joint-action space. Our exploration uses an interaction score: single-agent deviations are ranked by predicted gain, while two-agent deviations are sco...
tags: World Models, Exploration, and Open-Ended Reinforcement Learning

metadata.comment: Makes multi-agent MCTS tractable by proposing interaction-guided exploration over low-dimensional surrogate action representations with regret guarantees.
metadata.primary_topic_label: World Models, Exploration, and Open-Ended Reinforcement Learning

title: Scalable Learning in Structured Recurrent Spiking Neural Networks without Backpropagation
summary: Spiking Neural Networks (SNNs) provide a promising framework for energy-efficient and biologically grounded computation; however, scalable learning in deep recurrent architectures with sparse connectivity remains a major challenge. In this work, we propose a structured multi-layer recurrent SNN architecture composed of locally dense recurrent layers augmented with sparse small-world long-range projections to a readout population. The long-range connectivity is largely fixed, preserving routing efficiency and hardware scalability, while synaptic adaptation is performed using strictly local p...

metadata.comment: Proposes structured recurrent spiking networks trained without backprop using local three-factor plasticity, random feedback, and modulatory gating.

title: Affinity Is Not Enough: Recovering the Free Energy Principle in Mixture-of-Experts
summary: Broadly important today: Proposes MoE gate modifications with temporal memory, precision weighting, and anticipatory routing to fix routing failures at domain transitions.

metadata.comment: Proposes MoE gate modifications with temporal memory, precision weighting, and anticipatory routing to fix routing failures at domain transitions.
metadata.spotlight_comment: Broadly important today: Proposes MoE gate modifications with temporal memory, precision weighting, and anticipatory routing to fix routing failures at domain transitions.

title: Being-H0.7: A Latent World-Action Model from Egocentric Videos
summary: World-action modeling for robotics from egocentric video is a broadly active frontier topic and likely belongs in the daily hotspot feed as well.
tags: World Models, Exploration, and Open-Ended Reinforcement Learning

metadata.comment: Trains a latent world-action model that aligns future-informed posterior latent states with deployable prior states, avoiding pixel rollouts.
metadata.primary_topic_label: World Models, Exploration, and Open-Ended Reinforcement Learning
metadata.spotlight_comment: World-action modeling for robotics from egocentric video is a broadly active frontier topic and likely belongs in the daily hotspot feed as well.

title: Budget Constraints as Riemannian Manifolds
summary: Broadly important today: Treats exact budget constraints as a Riemannian manifold, enabling first-order optimization for quantization and MoE pruning under hard budgets.

metadata.comment: Treats exact budget constraints as a Riemannian manifold, enabling first-order optimization for quantization and MoE pruning under hard budgets.
metadata.spotlight_comment: Broadly important today: Treats exact budget constraints as a Riemannian manifold, enabling first-order optimization for quantization and MoE pruning under hard budgets.

title: SAGA: Workflow-Atomic Scheduling for AI Agent Inference on GPU Clusters
summary: Compound AI serving and workflow-aware scheduling are broadly important infrastructure themes likely to matter beyond this niche.

metadata.comment: Workflow-atomic GPU scheduling exploits cross-step KV-cache reuse and introduces a fairness metric for compound agent inference.
metadata.spotlight_comment: Compound AI serving and workflow-aware scheduling are broadly important infrastructure themes likely to matter beyond this niche.

title: Spiking Sequence Machines and Transformers
summary: The attempt to unify transformer and spiking sequence computation as the same primitive feels like a genuinely new conceptual direction.

metadata.comment: Provides a formal functional correspondence between spiking sequence machines and transformers through shared similarity-based retrieval and positional structure.
metadata.spotlight_comment: The attempt to unify transformer and spiking sequence computation as the same primitive feels like a genuinely new conceptual direction.

title: State Stream Transformer (SST) V2: Parallel Training of Nonlinear Recurrence for Latent Space Reasoning
summary: The paper proposes an unusual architectural direction—parallel-trainable nonlinear recurrence inside decoder layers—that could open a new line of model design.

metadata.comment: Introduces a transformer with streamed nonlinear recurrent latent state and a parallel training procedure for latent-space deliberation.
metadata.spotlight_comment: The paper proposes an unusual architectural direction—parallel-trainable nonlinear recurrence inside decoder layers—that could open a new line of model design.

title: Trees to Flows and Back: Unifying Decision Trees and Diffusion Models
summary: The tree–diffusion unification feels like a genuinely new conceptual direction rather than a routine extension.

metadata.comment: Unifies decision trees and diffusion models through a shared optimization principle, linking boosting-style hierarchy with continuous generative dynamics.
metadata.spotlight_comment: The tree–diffusion unification feels like a genuinely new conceptual direction rather than a routine extension.

title: Quoting Anthropic
summary:
We used an automatic classifier which judged sycophancy by looking at whether Claude showed a willingness to push back, maintain positions when challenged, give praise proportional to the merit of ideas, and speak frankly regardless of what a person wants to hear. Most of the time in these situations, Claude expressed no sycophancy—only 9% of conversations included sycophantic behavior (Figure 2). But two domains were exceptions: we saw sycophantic behavior in 38% of conversations focused on spirituality, and 25% of conversations on relationships.

— Anthropic, How people ask Claude for personal...
